page: Amazon Web Services g5.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, configured with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 2 threads per core under a dedicated allocation model. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between general-purpose CPU capabilities and GPU-driven workloads. The CPU performance is relatively modest, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 10 percent, and Geekbench multi-core scores in the bottom 25 percent. Memory bandwidth tests also register in the bottom 25 percent for both cached and uncached reads. However, the integrated NVIDIA A10G GPU delivers exceptional performance for machine learning tasks. LLM inference speeds for both small and medium models rank in the top 10 percent for prompt processing and text generation, with the 7B model achieving 2889.75 tokens per second for prompt processing and 94.25 tokens per second for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.xlarge represents a specialized entry point for GPU-accelerated computing. While the CPU and memory resources are modest, the inclusion of a dedicated 22 GB VRAM GPU and local NVMe storage provides a highly dense resource profile for targeted workloads without the cost of larger multi-GPU instances. This hardware configuration is highly suited for low-to-medium scale LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, and machine learning model deployment, whereas it is less optimal for CPU-bound compilation, heavy database operations, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. It includes 250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. While synthetic CPU benchmarks show modest performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores in the lower percentiles, the instance excels in machine learning tasks. Benchmark results place its LLM inference speeds for 135M and 7B models in the top 10 percent for both prompt processing and text generation. This makes the g5.xlarge a cost-effective choice for GPU-dependent workloads such as small-to-medium language model inference and graphics rendering, though it is less suited for CPU-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU and local NVMe storage, optimized for machine learning inference workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory
250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium-sized models
Suited for machine learning deployment, inference, and graphics rendering
